commit.message:
Add 42 markdown parser tests for blockquotes and edge cases

Blockquote tests (13): basic, multi-line, without space after >,
  followed by paragraph/heading/bullet/code/table, interleaved,
  complex document, streaming append, incremental append, empty
  quote, leading spaces, Hebrew/emoji content, pooled parity.

Edge case tests (29): paragraph with special chars, heading with
  trailing hashes and inline formatting, mixed bullet markers,
  non-sequential numbered lists, deeply nested bullets, table empty
  cells, tables with inline formatting/alignment/varying columns,
  code blocks with backticks in content/indented fences/consecutive
  languages, underscore/spaced/long horizontal rules, incremental
  append mid-code-block and block-kind change, mixed content with
  all block types.

Also align test project Avalonia version to 12.0.0-rc1.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: Strata
repository.description: A modern, token-driven Avalonia UI theme for professional desktop applications. Light, Dark, and High Contrast variants with 30+ custom controls.

commit.message:
Fix markdown: blockquotes, links in bold/italic, text foreground

- Add blockquote (>) support: MdBlockKind.Blockquote, parser detection,
  buffer-based accumulation, CreateBlockquoteControl with left accent bar
- Fix links inside bold/italic: AppendNestedCodeInlines now parses
  [link](url) syntax and propagates hasLinks for event handler wiring
- Fix text foreground: change default from Brush.TextSecondary to
  Brush.TextPrimary for proper contrast parity with standard TextBlock
- Add blockquote examples to demo markdown showcase
- Align demo Avalonia package versions to 12.0.0-rc1

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: build complete College Club & Event Management System

- database/db_manager.py: SQLite DatabaseManager with all CRUD ops,
  schema for users/clubs/events/participation/attendance/payments/
  permissions/tasks, sha256 password hashing, seeded admin account
- models/: OOP wrappers (User, Club, Event, Participation, Attendance,
  Payment, Permission, Task) each delegating to DatabaseManager
- utils/validators.py: regex validators for email, password, username,
  date, time, UPI ID, card number
- utils/helpers.py: format_date, format_datetime, generate_pass_number,
  truncate, status_color, role_display
- gui/app.py: Main Tk App with frame manager and show_frame()
- gui/login_screen.py: Login + Registration with full validation
- gui/admin_dashboard.py: Sidebar nav, stats cards, club/event/user
  management, analytics with per-event participant counts
- gui/role_dashboard.py: Club Head/Event Head/Dept Head/Member dash
  with clubs, events, attendance, tasks, permissions sections
- gui/student_dashboard.py: Browse events, register, view passes,
  attendance history, club join/leave
- gui/club_management.py: Club panel + MemberListDialog
- gui/event_management.py: EventDialog for create/edit/delete events
- gui/event_registration.py: Registration popup + digital pass viewer
- gui/attendance_validation.py: Hierarchical validation with
  role/permission checks and live count updates
- gui/payment_screen.py: Mock payment with card/UPI/cash flows,
  input validation, 95% success simulation
- gui/work_tracking.py: Task assignment (hierarchical), status updates,
  progress bar, task list
- gui/search_screen.py: Keyword + date-range + club + status filters
- main.py: Entry point with headless-display graceful error handling

Build complete .NET 9 WPF Steam Server Management application

- SteamServerTool.Core (net9.0): Models and Services class library
  - ServerConfig, RconConfig, ScheduledRconCommand models with camelCase JSON
  - ServerManager: start/stop/restart, crash detection, config load/save
  - BackupService: timestamped zip backup/restore/prune
  - SteamCmdService: steamcmd wrapper for install/update
  - RconClient: Source Engine RCON TCP protocol implementation
- SteamServerTool (net9.0-windows WPF): Main application
  - DarkTheme.xaml matching Arbiter VS Code dark palette
  - Full MainWindow with toolbar, server sidebar, tabbed detail panel
  - Overview/Config/Mods/Backups/Console/Logs tabs
  - Status indicator dots (green/yellow/red/amber)
  - DispatcherTimer-based real-time status refresh
  - RCON console with connect/disconnect/send
  - Operation log panel at bottom
- SteamServerTool.Tests (net9.0): xUnit test suite
  - 29 tests covering ServerConfig validation and ServerManager
  - All tests pass

commit.message:
feat(Frame): auto-hide back button on mobile, wire system back gesture

On platforms with a native back gesture (Android, iOS), the visual back
button is hidden via OnFormFactor and navigation is handled by
subscribing to TopLevel.BackRequestedEvent. This follows the same
pattern as MasterDetailsNavigator.RequiresBackButton().

The system back handler executes BackCommand when available and marks the
event as handled; if no BackCommand is set, the event bubbles up to
allow the OS default behavior (e.g., closing the app).

Desktop platforms retain the visual back button as before.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test(runtime): add comprehensive wire protocol test coverage (#344)

Expand the wire.rs test module from 6 tests to 55, covering all major
behaviours with roundtrip, boundary, and negative/error-path tests.

New coverage areas:
- Varint: zero, 127/128 boundary, u64::MAX, empty buffer, truncated
  multibyte, non-canonical 10th byte
- Zigzag: 0, ±1, i64::MIN/MAX, sign→parity mapping invariant
- Fixed32/64: encode+decode roundtrip, truncated buffer rejection
- HBF header: zero/max payload, null data, too-short buffer, wrong
  magic, wrong version, unknown flags, flag stripping, write_hbf_header
- Decode bytes: truncated payload
- Decode string: roundtrip, empty, truncated, empty buffer
- Decode tag: roundtrip, empty buffer
- Skip field: all four wire types, truncated fixed32/64, unknown type
- buf_has_remaining, buf_reset
- Envelope: empty payload, max msg_type boundary, negative msg_type,
  too-large msg_type, truncated mid-field, unknown wire type

Sabotage-validated three error-path tests to confirm they detect
regressions when the corresponding production checks are removed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Address code review: fix redundant check, smooth damage scaling, use userData for shield detection, use bad flag for negative rewards

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Implement 10 major gameplay system changes

1. Hard collision lock - army can't cross center wall dividers
2. Difficulty system - 2-3x enemy counts, within-segment HP scaling, higher boss HP
3. Negative choices - segments 3/4/7 have negative safe rewards forcing risk decisions
4. Weapon barrel system - collectible weapon barrels (assault/shotgun/minigun/rocket)
5. Map variation system - 5 layout patterns randomized per run
6. Army size control - soft cap 50, hard cap 100, diminishing returns
7. Companion combat fix - dragons/turrets/drones deal actual damage
8. Game variety - 6 extra segment types mixed into cycles 2+
9. Enemy variety - 4 new types (shield/jumping/ranged/charger)
10. Balance tuning - scroll speed 15, start 3 soldiers, medic 8s interval

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix age form submission - use URL-encoded POST for Apps Script CORS

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add project context detection to agent prompt

Auto-detect project language from manifest files (go.mod, package.json,
Cargo.toml, etc.) and include it in the system prompt so the AI model
generates code in the correct language.

- New context.go: DetectProjectContext() gathers language, manifests,
  .gitignore, and file tree
- Enriched system prompt explicitly requires code in detected language
- Structured user prompt with clear sections for all context
- Replace 'git add .' with targeted staging of only planned files
- Comprehensive tests for detection, prompt building, and staging

Closes #11

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(analyzers): CVJ-003 now replaces declaration type with bool

The code fix for CVJ-003 replaced .Validate(...) with .EvaluateSchema()
but left explicit variable declaration types (e.g. ValidationContext)
unchanged, producing code that would not compile.

Both fix paths (simple and collector-based) now detect
LocalDeclarationStatementSyntax with an explicit type and replace it
with bool. Added 3 new tests covering explicit type replacement,
collector + type replacement, and var-type preservation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: improve language detection using title field as primary indicator

25 more postings corrected where English text had Danish footer boilerplate,
causing the character-ratio heuristic to incorrectly classify as Danish.

as fallback. Result: 83 Danish, 83 English postings.

commit.message:
Add debug binaries and CBCBOX_VERBOSE support (v2.928)

- Build system (setup.py): include a debug binary in every wheel by default
  - x86_64: debug+AVX2 (cbc_dist_debug_avx2, -O1 -g -march=haswell)
  - other arches: plain debug (cbc_dist_debug, -O1 -g)
  - Only COIN-OR stack (CoinUtils/Osi/Clp/Cgl/Cbc) gets debug flags;
    OpenBLAS and AMD are always built optimised without debug symbols
  - Both debug dirs are bundled into the wheel when present
  - CBCBOX_BUILD_VARIANT={generic,avx2} still build without debug

- Runtime (__init__.py): CBCBOX_BUILD=debug now auto-selects the right
  debug variant (debug_avx2 on x86_64, debug on other architectures)

- Runtime (__init__.py): new CBCBOX_VERBOSE=1 env var prints the
  selected variant, binary path, lib dir and bundled libraries on every
  dispatch (previously only printed when CBCBOX_BUILD was explicitly set)

- Fix _print_build_info variant detection: check _debug_avx2 before
  _avx2 since the suffix _debug_avx2 also ends with _avx2

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Replace 5-level system with 9-segment endless loop

- Replace LEVEL_DEFS_3D with SEGMENT_DEFS (9 segments with fixed SAFE/RISK rewards)
- Add ENV_PALETTES for environment variation per cycle
- Add BOSS_HP definitions for ogre and fireDragon boss types
- Redesign startGame() to not take level parameter; always starts at segment 1
- Add segmentCycle, difficultyMult, and milestone tracking
- Build internal segment sequence: gates+enemies for normal, boss+recovery for bosses
- SAFE gates (left) give fixed soldier bonuses; RISK gates (right) give upgrades
- Boss segments use proper enemy types (ogre/fireDragon) instead of tank
- After segment 9 boss: increment cycle, increase difficulty +0.4, continue from segment 1
- Display cycle transition message overlay
- Update HUD to show segment info instead of level number
- Update lose screen to show milestone and cycle info
- Preserve all Three.js infrastructure (renderer, lights, road, trees, input, etc.)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Eliminate auto-save restore race with synchronous localStorage read

All previous restore approaches had race conditions between async
localStorage read, component rendering, and Monaco editor initialization.

Fix: use IJSInProcessRuntime.Invoke<T> (available in Blazor WASM) to
read localStorage synchronously in OnInitialized(), before the component
ever renders. The state is fully resolved before editors are created,
so no SetValue push is needed — editors get the correct content on
their initial render via the model (schemaFiles, GetInitialUserCode).

Removed: hasRestoredState flag, OnInitializedAsync override, all
OnDidInit/OnAfterRenderAsync restore logic.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: Corvus.Text.Json
repository.description: A version of System.Text.Json to support schema-based code generation.

commit.message:
Fix auto-save restore: use OnDidInit callbacks instead of OnAfterRenderAsync

The Blazor @ref is assigned during render, but the Monaco JS editor
is created asynchronously after that. Checking 'schemaEditor is not null'
in OnAfterRenderAsync can run before Monaco is ready (editors.length: 0).

Move the restore push into OnSchemaEditorInit and OnUserCodeEditorInit
callbacks, which fire when Monaco has actually created the editor instance.
This guarantees SetValue is safe to call.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: MCP server for agent–task interaction (#23)

* Initial plan

* feat: add MCP server for agent-task interaction

Expose Agent Board tasks via the Model Context Protocol (MCP) so that
external agents can list, read, and update tasks through a stdio-based
JSON-RPC server.

New files:
- src/mcp/mcpTypes.ts — pure MCP protocol type definitions
- src/mcp/mcpToolHandler.ts — pure tool handler (list_tasks, get_task, update_task)
- src/mcp/mcpServer.ts — standalone stdio MCP server entry point
- src/test/suite/mcpToolHandler.test.ts — 30 unit tests

Config:
- Added mcp section to ProjectConfigData and JSON schema
- Added npm run mcp script to package.json
